admitted.candidate_text: Kai scrubs the kitchen sink's drain with an old toothbrush, pressing hard enough to fray the bristles. Behind him, the unopened letter rests on the table, radiating the cold, salt-heavy air of the harbor docks. He focuses entirely on the rhythm of his hands, convincing himself that as long as he stays busy, his silence isn't an answer yet.

Kai scrubs the kitchen counter with methodical, punishing focus, working his way carefully around the unopened envelope. The rhythmic squeak of the sponge sounds too much like the gulls down by the docks, but he keeps his eyes locked on the damp granite. If he just gets the kitchen perfectly clean first, he tells himself, he will finally be ready to figure out what to say.

Kai turns his back to the kitchen table and begins scrubbing the already-clean cast iron skillet with meticulous, punishing focus. The envelope sits untouched behind him, its sharp white edges demanding an answer he isn't ready to give. He runs the faucet on high, letting the rush of water drown out the imagined sound of the harbor tide and the memory of her asking him to at least be honest in his silence.

Kai scrubs the kitchen counter with punishing exactness, keeping his back strictly to the table where the unopened envelope rests. He focuses entirely on the rhythmic squeak of the sponge, pretending he can't hear the distant foghorn from the harbor drifting through the open window. If he just keeps cleaning, maybe the heavy silence in the room won't feel like an answer he has already given.

Kai scrubs the already clean grout around the kitchen sink, pressing the sponge down until his knuckles turn white. Behind him, the unopened envelope rests exactly where he left it on the table, its silence growing louder by the minute. He turns the faucet on full blast, letting the rushing water drown out the imagined sound of harbor gulls and the memory of her demanding an honest silence.

Kai disassembles the coffee maker with surgical precision, laying each piece on a towel exactly three inches from the unopened envelope. He scrubs the glass carafe until it squeaks, a sharp sound that mimics the boats rubbing against the harbor docks in the evening tide. He tells himself he just needs to finish this one chore before he sits down to read it, letting the safe, heavy silence stretch out a little longer.

Kai empties the silverware drawer onto the counter, meticulously polishing each spoon to a mirror shine while the unopened envelope waits on the table behind him. The rhythmic squeak of the cloth is loud, but not loud enough to drown out the phantom sound of water lapping against the harbor pylons. He scrubs a water spot until his thumb aches, terrified that if he stops moving for even a second, his silence will solidify into a final answer.

Kai takes a stiff-bristled brush to the kitchen tile grout, scrubbing with a frantic, rhythmic precision that leaves his knuckles white. The unopened envelope sits just at the edge of his peripheral vision, its silence as heavy as the unspoken words that ruined their last night together. He focuses entirely on the abrasive scrape of the bristles, desperate to drown out the imagined sound of dark water lapping against the harbor docks.
